channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Karnataka requests Centre to raise ESI ceiling following minimum wage hike
item.description: The Karnataka government has formally urged the Central Government to revise the Employees’ State Insurance (ESI) ceiling from ₹21,000 per month to ₹30,000, following a substantial increase in minimum...

item.title: President Samia Suluhu Hassan appoints new chairs for government boards including PSSSF
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has made important appointments for three government boards in Tanzania. This includes naming new chairpersons to lead key institutions. According to the statement from C...

item.title: PNP confirms 11 deaths in Toboso clash had gunpowder traces
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported that 11 out of 19 people killed in a series of encounters in Barangay Salamanca, Toboso, Negros Occidental last April tested positive for gunpowder. This ...

item.title: Man Critically Injured in California Attack Near Pro-Trump Display; Suspect Reportedly Mentally Ill Navy Veteran
item.description: In Escondido, California, 69-year-old Kerry Sheron, known for his prominent pro-Trump home display, was critically injured in an assault near his residence. Police responded to the scene Wednesday aft...

item.title: Exploring Talent: Natural Gift Versus Years of Dedication in Sport
item.description: Justin Langer reflects on the nature of talent in sport, drawing on his 35 years of professional experience. He argues that talent is often misunderstood, with some believing it is purely innate, whil...

item.title: Albino Buffalo in Bangladesh Attracts Crowds Ahead of Eid Festival
item.description: A rare albino buffalo in Bangladesh has captured the attention of locals and visitors alike ahead of the Eid al-Adha festival. Known for its flowing blond hair and striking resemblance to former US Pr...

item.title: Big Oil Companies Renew Interest in Alaska Oil and Gas Projects Due to Middle East Crisis
item.description: Dis article talk about how big oil companies don begin to show fresh interest in drilling for oil and gas for Alaska, one place wey dem bin dey avoid before. Because of di crisis for Middle East wey d...

item.title: Study Shows Traffic Significantly Contributes to Urban Heat
item.description: Recent research highlights that urban traffic is a notable contributor to the rising temperatures in cities, a phenomenon often overlooked in urban planning. Known as the urban heat island effect, cit...

item.title: Petrol and Diesel Prices Decreased by Government Amid Global Fuel Market Changes
item.description: On May 23, 2026, the Government of Pakistan announced a reduction in petroleum prices, lowering petrol by Rs6 per litre to Rs403.78 and high-speed diesel (HSD) by Rs6.8 to Rs402.78. The Petroleum Divi...

item.title: Pakistan and China Celebrate 75 Years of Strong Bilateral Relations
item.description: Pakistan and China are celebrating 75 years of close diplomatic and strategic relations, often described as 'ironclad' friendship. The milestone comes amid global turbulence and regional challenges, y...

item.title: Tanzania Government Starts Big Tree Planting to Fight Deforestation
item.description: The Tanzania government has announced a big plan to restore the country's forests by planting two million trees every year in different regions. This move comes as the nation loses about 469,000 hecta...

item.title: PTI holds protests across Pakistan demanding Imran Khan's release and action against inflation
item.description: On Friday, workers and supporters of Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI) staged protests in different cities of the country including Islamabad, Peshawar and Lahore. In the federal capital, PTI Islamabad w...

item.title: Visayas faces repeated yellow alerts due to limited power capacity
item.description: Residents in the Visayas experienced another yellow alert as the region's power supply remained under strain for the fifth consecutive day. The National Grid Corp. of the Philippines (NGCP) raised the...

item.title: Candid Portraits of Françoise Hardy in Venice Captured by Steve Schapiro
item.description: In September 1966, American photojournalist Steve Schapiro took a series of candid photographs of French singer and fashion icon Françoise Hardy in Venice, Italy. Hardy was in Venice for the film fest...

item.title: Maruti Suzuki Announces Modest Price Increase Across Models Due to Rising Input Costs Starting June 2026
item.description: Maruti Suzuki, India's largest car manufacturer, has officially announced a price hike across its vehicle lineup effective from June 2026. According to a corporate filing, the company will increase pr...

item.title: AI-Generated Fan Songs Are Trending Ahead Of 2026 World Cup
item.description: As the 2026 World Cup dey approach, football fans don begin dey use artificial intelligence to create plenty fan-made songs wey dey trend online. This kind music dey celebrate teams like France, Brazi...

item.title: Oxford University develops experimental vaccine for rare Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak
item.description: Scientists at Oxford University are urgently developing a new vaccine aimed at the rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, which has no currently proven vaccine. The outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Co...

item.title: New York Times Reporter Highlights Somalia's Humanitarian Crisis Following Aid Cuts and Regional Conflicts
item.description: In a recent interview on CNN's Amanpour, New York Times reporter Peter Goodman shared his firsthand observations from a trip to Somalia, where the combined effects of the Iran war and significant cuts...

item.title: South Africa sees decline in murders but violent crime remains high
item.description: South Africa has reported a decrease in its national murder rate, with 546 fewer lives lost in the first quarter of 2026 compared with the same period last year. Police Minister Firoz Cachalia highlig...

item.title: TNT Tropang Giga looks for new import after Bol Bol suffers Achilles injury
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – TNT Tropang Giga is facing a major setback in the 2026 PBA Commissioner’s Cup semifinals after star import Bol Bol sustained a suspected partial Achilles tear. The 7-foot-3 forme...
